239747.explanation: For important issues that affect all residents, it must be possible to hold a referendum.

239748.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important to encourage resident initiatives.

239749.explanation: Elderly policy is important, but an alderman with only elderly policy in their portfolio makes the administration unnecessarily expensive. A portfolio holder for a specific group is undesirable; young people are important too, for example.

239750.explanation: With the extra income from tourist tax, Vlaardingen can be made more attractive.

239751.explanation: Staggered opening and closing times result in a more diverse offering and less nuisance.

239752.explanation: Vlaardingen already does a lot in promotion. ‘VlaarDINGEN doen’ is a good example of that. We currently invest sufficiently in promotion. Above all, a more attractive (city) center is important for tourism.

239753.explanation: A fine on vacancies prevents speculation and promotes lower rents for shops and opportunities for startups.

239754.explanation: Everyone deserves to be able to participate in Vlaardingen.

239755.explanation: The staff must be diverse. A nice mix of (cultural) backgrounds and ages. This does not require extra efforts specifically to attract older employees.

239757.explanation: In adverse weather conditions, the use of wood stoves and open fireplaces must be prohibited to prevent health complaints among Vlaardingen residents with asthma or COPD.

239758.explanation: Vlaardingers who want to improve the quality of public green spaces themselves deserve support.

239759.explanation: The municipality can pursue preferential policies for the establishment of sustainable companies. At the same time, we do not want to exclude companies outright, especially if they want to become more sustainable but need support.

239761.explanation: The municipality must cooperate in the construction of heat networks, but not contribute financially.

239762.explanation: The municipality is obliged to house a certain number of refugees with residence permits. Besides, asylum centers in the Netherlands would otherwise become overcrowded and expensive emergency shelters (such as in hotels) would be necessary. These people have only limited influence on the waiting lists for Vlaardingen residents.

239763.explanation: It is important that all newcomers can find their place in Vlaardingen society. This requires investment in language education and social participation. This also enables newcomers to more easily contribute to society.

239764.explanation: Mrija must remain open as long as necessary. As long as that terrible war continues.

239765.explanation: Vlaardingen must contribute its share to housing people fleeing war, persecution, and violence.

239767.explanation: MBO (vocational) education in Vlaardingen is very important to make the city attractive to young people. Vlaardingen really needs MBO students. This requires active lobbying.

239768.explanation: Providing equal opportunities in education requires additional effort for students who receive less support from their home environment. Homework guidance should not depend on the parents' income.

239769.explanation: Alcohol is harmful to health. Active discouragement is important, especially considering the importance of youth.

239771.explanation: There is no need for additional coffeeshops in Vlaardingen, but there is also no reason to ban them everywhere.

239772.explanation: Camera surveillance is an expensive tool that does not always prove effective for safety. Supervision by people is more effective. GroenLinks-PvdA prefers people on the street over cameras on the wall.

239773.explanation: Nuisance caused by speed-modified fatbikes must be tackled, and riders should be addressed more often for antisocial driving behavior.

239774.explanation: Preventive searches are now permitted in most parts of Vlaardingen. It was intended as a temporary emergency measure but has become fixed policy in many places. Preventive searches must be applied selectively because they infringe on civil rights.

239775.explanation: Promoting alcoholic beverages does not align with encouraging healthy behavior. Therefore, the ban should be better enforced.

239776.explanation: It is an expensive and pointless measure because additional deposits will not be prevented and it is easy to be unrecognizable on camera.

239777.explanation: Residents of Vlaardingen must wait years for social rental housing. Housing is becoming increasingly expensive, leaving many without alternatives. The municipality must prioritize creating sufficient affordable housing.

239778.explanation: Building higher residential towers in suitable locations is a good way to create additional housing. After all, we want to preserve green spaces, including the Broekpolder.

239779.explanation: Vacancy is a disgrace given the severe housing shortage. Converting offices into homes must therefore definitely be encouraged.

239780.explanation: It is important to promote turnover in the housing market and better combine living and care. The establishment of so-called 'knarrenhof' (courtyards for elderly) also deserves support.

239781.explanation: Adding housing in the center increases vibrancy and broadens support for amenities.

239782.explanation: New construction will still be needed after 2030. However, this should be realized within the built-up area because green spaces must remain outside of it.

239783.explanation: The Liesveldviaduct runs straight through the center, causing noise disturbance, pollution, and a hardened appearance. The viaduct is unnecessary to reach the center. Less asphalt and more peace and greenery make the inner city more attractive for shopping, staying, recreation, and living.

239784.explanation: Within the existing buildings, this industrial area offers the best opportunity to build homes. The companies there will need to be relocated. Therefore, this proposal will require a long implementation period.

239785.explanation: The municipality must ensure that everyone can participate in Vlaardingen and must therefore provide extra support for people with low incomes.

239786.explanation: Of course, it is good if people receiving social assistance do meaningful community work, and many do so. The problem is it cannot be mandatory, as for some people it is simply not possible or feasible to work due to their personal circumstances.

239787.explanation: Wage cost subsidies can encourage companies to employ long-term unemployed people or those with disabilities. In the long term, this investment pays off.

239788.explanation: Relatively many people live in poverty in Vlaardingen. Cancellation of municipal taxes is one of the few tools the municipality has to combat this poverty. That remains absolutely necessary.

239789.explanation: It is not useful to force people aged 55+ to apply for jobs. It only causes frustration. However, helping 55+ individuals find suitable jobs or meaningful volunteer work is useful.

239790.explanation: Just as there is care leave for people with jobs, there can also be exemption from job application obligations for social assistance recipients.

239791.explanation: Youth work in Vlaardingen is already under pressure. Further budget cuts will further erode the services. Cuts will only increase the risks of school dropout and crime in the long term.

239792.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports free public transport for all people with lower incomes (up to 120% of the social assistance norm), regardless of their age. Free public transport for all elderly regardless of income is an unfocused and expensive measure in our view.

239793.explanation: More space for greenery, pedestrians, and cyclists is important to us. Additionally, currently 80% of parking garages in the Vlaardingen center are empty. Parking in these garages should be made more attractive so people do not have to park on the street.

239794.explanation: In neighborhoods where parking pressure is too high, paid parking can offer a solution. The fee for resident permits must remain affordable.

239795.explanation: Fine dust and air pollution are major problems in our environment. Introducing an environmental zone to exclude polluting vehicles is one of the few ways to improve air quality and thus health in our city.

239796.explanation: The municipality must ensure sufficient charging points for electric cars. This promotes cleaner transport, which also improves air quality in Vlaardingen.

239797.explanation: Thirty kilometer zones demonstrably improve traffic safety. They also reduce emissions of harmful exhaust gases.

239798.explanation: It depends on what these parking spaces are intended for. For company vehicles that may be desirable, but it must not come at the expense of green or playground facilities.

239799.explanation: A water bus stop would be a nice addition to the public transport options in Vlaardingen.

239800.explanation: A night metro (especially on weekends) would increase accessibility and attractiveness of Vlaardingen as a residential city.

239801.explanation: For GroenLinks-PvdA, the bicycle is the most important means of transport (Bike First), and this definitely includes free guarded bicycle parking facilities. Also at the metro stations.

239802.explanation: Raising the permitted maximum speed is undesirable because higher speeds demonstrably lead to more accidents.

239803.explanation: The number of homeless people has increased considerably recently, including in Vlaardingen. The municipality has a duty of care for these vulnerable people.

239804.explanation: A community center brings people together and provides space for neighborhood initiatives. Every neighborhood in Vlaardingen deserves a community center.

239805.explanation: It is not about allocating money but about delivering good (Social Support Act) care such as home care and transportation. More money is therefore not always the solution. The municipality is not responsible for much care, such as nursing and health care.

239807.explanation: Youth care must be better and more effective. It is questionable whether allocating more money is the solution. A better approach, emphasizing prevention and early detection, is more important, with extra attention to the children who need it most.

239808.explanation: This temporary care is also called respite care. It is a good idea to allocate funds for it because it can delay or prevent admissions. It is also the much-needed recognition for those who care for their loved ones.

239747.explanation: It is good if people can express their opinion, but enough people must participate in the referendum. In previous referendums, very few people participated. Then it is a waste of time.

239748.explanation: The VVD supports letting people take matters into their own hands. Funding should be made available for this. The question is whether the money should be given directly to people (to prevent misuse) or whether it is better provided through the municipality (in the form of labor, tools, or plants).

239749.explanation: This is unworkable. Why have an alderman for the elderly but not one for the youth? Or an alderman for anything else? Too many aldermen (and Vlaardingen already has 5, which is quite a lot) makes governance unfeasible and costs Vlaardingen too much money.

239750.explanation: Tourists help the city and entrepreneurs in Vlaardingen by spending money when they are here. The Vlaardingen VVD wants to attract more tourists to Vlaardingen and not scare them away with high tourist taxes.

239751.explanation: Entrepreneurs in Vlaardingen should have more freedom to do what they do best. But that is only possible if local residents do not experience nuisance. So, 'Yes, provided that'.

239752.explanation: It is always good to present Vlaardingen positively and thereby attract more tourists. Tourists bring atmosphere to the city and help entrepreneurs (hotels, cafes, restaurants, and retailers) through extra income.

239753.explanation: This measure does not help fill shops. The Vlaardingen VVD wants to focus more on supporting starting entrepreneurs.

239754.explanation: The Vlaardingen VVD wants to help more people get to work. Working pays off. Anything that helps people move from social assistance to paid work is positive. Stroomopwaarts does this for the municipality.

239755.explanation: For the VVD, age is not decisive. It is important that the municipality hires people with the right knowledge and experience. These qualities are often found among those aged 55 and older as well. If someone is suitable for the position, they should be hired regardless of age.

239757.explanation: Residents of Vlaardingen should be able to continue using a fireplace or wood stove. But one person’s enjoyment must not become another's problem. Owners must therefore take nuisance to the neighborhood into account, but we will not prohibit it.

239758.explanation: Through individual initiative, we keep the neighborhood clean and livable.

239759.explanation: Vlaardingen needs more entrepreneurs and companies that provide employment but also internship opportunities. If Vlaardingen does not want businesses, there are many other municipalities that will welcome them with open arms.

239761.explanation: No one can say exactly how much money is needed, and the municipality cannot take that financial risk. Moreover, such experiments should not lead to high costs for the residents of Vlaardingen.

239762.explanation: Everyone in Vlaardingen has the right to housing. We use a fair housing allocation system.

239763.explanation: Money can only be spent once and must be distributed fairly. Provide jobs for newcomers and offer courses where they truly learn the language and Dutch norms.

239764.explanation: The Ukraine village is an agreement made by the municipality. Vlaardingen adheres to agreements made. Ukrainians contribute to society by working and such. The village must not block the construction of the new water treatment plant.

239765.explanation: Vlaardingen must comply with the law, and all council members have sworn to uphold the law. Any party that says Vlaardingen should not accommodate asylum seekers is making a statement that is completely impossible. Voter deceit! But preferably close it as soon as possible.

239767.explanation: Vlaardingen as an MBO (secondary vocational education) city is developing, and that includes efforts to bring more programs to Vlaardingen.

239768.explanation: Education is the future. This helps young people continue learning and enter the workforce.

239769.explanation: The VVD is against patronizing. Sports clubs should decide this themselves. A drink after a match should be allowed as long as it remains sociable. The income from the canteen is important for many sports clubs to continue existing. Attention to alcohol abuse is appropriate, though.

239771.explanation: We have enough coffee shops and want to prevent further expansion. The same applies to, for example, hair salons and fast-food outlets.

239772.explanation: In addition to more enforcement officers, camera surveillance helps increase the safety of Vlaardingers. Cameras deter and help, when necessary, to identify perpetrators afterwards.

239773.explanation: The municipality must enforce regulations! Fatbikes do not belong on sidewalks and in shopping areas. If banning them is not possible, then as much control as possible is needed to ensure fatbike users also follow the rules.

239774.explanation: Weapons are repeatedly found through searches. By removing those weapons from the street through searches, Vlaardingen becomes safer.

239775.explanation: The municipality should not interfere with prices in the hospitality sector. That is the entrepreneurs' responsibility. However, the law applies and forbids price promotions. So, whether we like it or not: the municipality cannot override Dutch law.

239776.explanation: The Vlaardingen VVD supports more camera surveillance. This helps keep the city livable and clean and increases the sense of safety.

239777.explanation: The Vlaardingen housing stock must be balanced. We need to focus on multiple segments, such as affordable owner-occupied and mid-range rental homes.

239778.explanation: Vlaardingen has little space left for building. The places where housing construction can still take place must be used as efficiently as possible. If possible and if it fits the environment, tall apartment buildings like Hoogwitte should be built there to help solve the housing shortage.

239779.explanation: The municipality should stimulate developers, but not subsidize them!

239780.explanation: More homes, both rental and owner-occupied, must be built for all target groups. Also for the elderly, as this creates flow that makes homes available for families, etc.

239781.explanation: More homes in the city center partially solve the housing shortage and help entrepreneurs because more residents mean more earning opportunities!

239782.explanation: New homes will always be needed, especially in the coming years. Anyone who supports this statement does not want to solve the housing shortage.

239783.explanation: The Liesveldviaduct is not the most attractive place in our city. It is good to address this viaduct. We will reroute the traffic to increase livability in the city, make the city center more attractive, and attract more visitors.

239784.explanation: Entrepreneurs in Vlaardingen already have too little space and would like to expand. It would be different if the industrial area was empty, but that is not the case.

239785.explanation: In the coming years, we will have to make savings. We also do this by helping more people find work. More and higher benefits do not help with this.

239786.explanation: Everyone must participate in Vlaardingen. Ideally, everyone should work. But at the very least, people are expected to remain involved in society by helping where needed and possible. This also makes it easier for people to take a step toward employment.

239787.explanation: The Vlaardingen VVD supports helping unemployed and long-term unemployed people find jobs. The municipality can assist by stimulating (participation) jobs.

239788.explanation: Writing off debts is not the solution to lifting people out of poverty. Debt counseling and working are.

239789.explanation: Parties that agree with this write off people aged 55 and older. The business community is eager for people and cannot fill vacancies. Age is not important here, but knowledge and skills are.

239790.explanation: Providing informal care is important. Someone who provides informal care one or two days a week can also certainly work (partly).

239791.explanation: Youth work helps prevent early school leaving.

239792.explanation: Realizing this for all elderly people is very expensive and we do not have the money for it. Moreover, not all elderly people need this.

239793.explanation: We must also make use of the parking garages, which are largely empty.

239794.explanation: The VVD opposes paid parking but also looks at reality. Sometimes it cannot be avoided because residents must be able to park in their neighborhood near their homes. But only after considering all other measures first. Paid parking is a last resort.

239795.explanation: Environmental zones hit people hard financially and prevent Vlaardingers from being able to reach the city center.

239796.explanation: Charging points are important but must remain proportional to other parking spaces. The parking pressure in Vlaardingen is already high.

239797.explanation: 30 km/h is fine in places where dangerous situations occur or around schools. But in large parts of the city, 30 km/h is not necessary at all and 50 km/h works perfectly.

239798.explanation: This is a good idea to reduce parking pressure in the neighborhoods.

239799.explanation: This is a good addition to regular public transport and can also help attract tourists visiting Rotterdam to Vlaardingen.

239800.explanation: The VVD supports this. But safety in the metro and at and around stations must also be ensured.

239801.explanation: This promotes bicycle use and prevents bike theft. In addition, it provides an opportunity for people to get used to a job again through Stroomopwaarts so they can move on to a regular job.

239802.explanation: When reducing the speed to 30 km/h where roads are dangerous, roads where a higher speed is possible should also be allowed. The Burgemeester Heusdenslaan is an example where that is possible.

239803.explanation: Vlaardingen is currently a central municipality, which means we have to accommodate many homeless people who actually do not live in Vlaardingen. We believe the entire region has a responsibility in this.

239804.explanation: This is the wish of one neighborhood, and different neighborhoods will have different wishes.

239805.explanation: People who really need care can always count on the VVD. However, we must be critical of the agreements made with care providers and make clear agreements about costs to prevent misuse and excess leading to higher taxes for Vlaardingen residents.

239807.explanation: At this moment, the municipality receives extra money from the national government for youth care. That is currently sufficient to pay for the care. Therefore, additional municipal funds are not needed now. If the demand for youth care increases in the future and national funding is no longer enough, the municipality must reassess whether additional funds are necessary.

239808.explanation: Informal caregiving is a good solution for the overburdened care system in the Netherlands. Informal caregivers should be supported in their important role wherever possible.

239747.explanation: We support the (binding) referendum. For major and impactful decisions, residents should be able to directly participate in discussions and decision-making. This strengthens local democracy and keeps politics close to the people of Vlaardingen.

239748.explanation: Residents often know better than the municipality what their neighborhood needs. That is why ONS.Vlaardingen wants to support residents who come up with good plans with budgets and space to implement improvements themselves.

239749.explanation: A dedicated alderman for the elderly can be a means to provide more focus and attention to senior policy. ONS.Vlaardingen believes the interests of seniors should be structurally and visibly included, with clear responsibility.

239750.explanation: ONS.Vlaardingen prefers to cut costs within its own organization rather than increase taxes. If a choice must be made, a tourist tax affects Vlaardingers the least. Any increase should never be automatic.

239751.explanation: Entrepreneurs know best what works for their business. ONS.Vlaardingen chooses freedom in opening hours, with one clear requirement: act responsibly towards the environment and actively limit nuisances. Freedom goes hand in hand with responsibility.

239752.explanation: The campaign “Kom VlaarDINGEN Doen!” already receives structural funding. ONS.Vlaardingen sees that this works. Additional budget is not needed. It's about smart use and leveraging what we already have.

239753.explanation: Vacancy harms the city center. ONS.Vlaardingen believes owners are responsible for their properties. Those who leave properties vacant for a long time must be addressed and fined if necessary. A well-maintained city center requires active effort.

239754.explanation: Everyone on social assistance must be helped towards sustainable employment. This saves society costs and gives people perspective, self-worth and financial independence. If additional resources demonstrably help more people out of social assistance, ONS.Vlaardingen supports this.

239755.explanation: The municipality must select based on quality and suitability, not on age. ONS.Vlaardingen stands for equal opportunities: everyone should be assessed on competencies and commitment, regardless of background, gender or age.

239757.explanation: Residents decide for themselves how to heat their homes. ONS.Vlaardingen opposes patronizing bans. In case of nuisance, consultation and advice are appropriate, not general restrictions for everyone.

239758.explanation: By managing green spaces themselves, residents take responsibility for their neighborhood. ONS.Vlaardingen chooses ownership of public spaces and encourages active Vlaardingers who want to improve their neighborhood.

239759.explanation: Vlaardingen must be open to all entrepreneurs who bring jobs and economic power. We will not drive companies away with moral barriers. A good business climate and employment are top priorities.

239761.explanation: District heating systems lock residents into one system with high costs and little control. ONS.Vlaardingen chooses affordability and freedom of choice. The municipality should not spend money on this measure.

239762.explanation: Status holders must not be given priority for social housing. Vlaardingers with local ties and/or who have been on the waiting list for years should come first. It cannot be that people who have just arrived in the Netherlands go ahead of residents who have waited for years.

239763.explanation: The municipality should not spend extra money on this. Integration costs must not be passed on to Vlaardingers. ONS.Vlaardingen believes the national government is responsible for this and municipal resources should go to our own residents and neighborhoods.

239764.explanation: ONS.Vlaardingen wants Mrija to become available to Vlaardingers as soon as possible. Temporary shelter must not become a permanent solution. These homes are urgently needed to house our own young people and housing seekers.

239765.explanation: ONS.Vlaardingen opposes shelter at Zuidbuurtseweg and has done so from the beginning. The pressure on the city and neighborhoods is too great. Vlaardingen has already taken ample responsibility. It is a gross disgrace that especially the Westwijk, which is already under enough pressure, must bear the burdens again.

239767.explanation: Vlaardingen should be a strong MBO (secondary vocational education) city. ONS.Vlaardingen wants more educational programs, including a crafts school, so young people can learn a trade with their hands and build a future here.

239768.explanation: ONS.Vlaardingen finds it important that children get good opportunities. Targeted support after school can help reduce disadvantages, provided it is used effectively and truly benefits education.

239769.explanation: Sports clubs and their volunteers can take responsibility here themselves very well. ONS.Vlaardingen opposes patronizing and believes that having a drink after the match should simply be allowed.

239771.explanation: Extra coffee shops increase the risk of nuisance and insecurity. ONS.Vlaardingen believes Vlaardingen already has enough and that expansion is not in the best interest of the city.

239772.explanation: Camera surveillance increases the chance of detection and works preventively. ONS.Vlaardingen wants to use this to intervene more quickly in nuisance and crime. Especially where enforcement falls short. Safety requires visible measures.

239773.explanation: Fatbikers often behave as if the street belongs to them. ONS.Vlaardingen wants more controls, speed measurements and immediate intervention. Those who ride dangerously will lose their fatbike. The safety of others comes first.

239774.explanation: Preventive frisking works and removes weapons from the streets. Those who have nothing to hide are finished within minutes. Every knife less is a potentially saved life. Safety comes first.

239775.explanation: The hospitality industry should be able to determine its own prices. ONS.Vlaardingen supports fewer rules and more personal responsibility. This works without problems in surrounding cities as well. Patronizing is not necessary here.

239776.explanation: Illegal dumping of waste is no longer an incident but a habit for a small group. ONS.Vlaardingen deploys cameras near containers to stop these polluters and restore cleanliness to the neighborhood. Enough is enough.

239777.explanation: ONS.Vlaardingen stands for a good balance in the housing stock. Social rental housing remains necessary so that residents of Vlaardingen can live in the city regardless of their income. At the same time, we safeguard the mix so that Vlaardingen remains livable and balanced.

239778.explanation: ONS.Vlaardingen wants high-rise buildings only in locations where they fit, such as the city center and well-connected sites. Not in the middle of low-rise neighborhoods. Quality and integration always take precedence over quantity.

239779.explanation: ONS.Vlaardingen wants to utilize vacant properties. Converting offices into homes speeds up housing construction, prevents decay, and gives existing buildings a new, useful purpose.

239780.explanation: ONS.Vlaardingen wants more suitable homes for seniors, such as 55+ apartments and living arrangements like a knarrenhof. This promotes housing turnover and ensures that Vlaardingen residents of all ages can continue living in the city.

239781.explanation: The city center is already under pressure due to a lack of facilities, such as parking spaces. Adding more homes without first improving those facilities puts entrepreneurs under further strain. ONS.Vlaardingen chooses a livable and accessible center.

239782.explanation: Vlaardingen must continue to develop. ONS.Vlaardingen wants to keep building after 2030, so residents of all ages and incomes can find appropriate housing in our city. Importantly, we never want to build in the Broekpolder.

239783.explanation: The Liesveld viaduct is a crucial connection for accessibility and traffic flow. ONS.Vlaardingen opposes its closure and chooses a city that remains accessible for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs.

239784.explanation: Groot Vettenoord could become a residential area, but only carefully. ONS.Vlaardingen wants to make agreements with the companies located there and explore suitable alternative locations together, so that housing construction becomes possible without losing jobs.

239785.explanation: Timely support prevents larger problems and higher costs later on. ONS.Vlaardingen chooses to help those in need because prevention is cheaper and more humane than repairing afterward.

239786.explanation: Those receiving social assistance benefits should give something back. Socially valuable work contributes to structure, self-esteem, and involvement in the city.

239787.explanation: Subsidized jobs have not proven effective and often lead to abuse and temporary work without prospects. ONS.Vlaardingen chooses sustainable jobs, not artificial constructions.

239788.explanation: Those with little to spend should not be taxed extra. ONS.Vlaardingen chooses solidarity and will continue to allow exemptions for residents with a low income.

239789.explanation: People over 55 can and should continue participating in the labor market where possible. ONS.Vlaardingen chooses commitment and perspective, with tailored solutions where needed.

239790.explanation: ONS.Vlaardingen chooses customized solutions. Those who provide intensive informal care, sometimes day and night, should be exempt from the job application obligation. The amount of care delivered is decisive. This is humane and saves society money.

239791.explanation: Youth work is not a cost but an investment in safety, development, and prevention. It helps prevent disturbances and offers young people structure, guidance, and prospects. Less youth work means more problems on the streets.

239792.explanation: Free public transport for the elderly increases independence and mobility. ONS.Vlaardingen believes that growing old in Vlaardingen should not yield less than in Rotterdam. Our seniors deserve this.

239793.explanation: Parking space in the city center is already too limited. ONS.Vlaardingen wants residents to be able to park and therefore advocates returning parking garages to municipal control.

239794.explanation: Paid parking can only be introduced with residents' consent via a referendum. Although this has been abolished, we want to reintroduce it. ONS.Vlaardingen also prioritizes adding parking spaces first to keep the city accessible.

239795.explanation: An environmental zone imposes costs on residents and entrepreneurs and hardly has any effect. With polluting shipping passing by our city and Pernis across the water, such a measure is mainly symbolic politics.

239796.explanation: Charging points belong where they are needed, but preferably by adding extra spots rather than replacing existing parking places. ONS.Vlaardingen chooses tailored solutions without sacrificing parking spaces.

239797.explanation: One general 30 km/h speed limit for all of Vlaardingen is nonsensical. ONS.Vlaardingen chooses tailored solutions: we slow down where necessary, and where possible, you should be able to drive through.

239798.explanation: Additional parking spaces on the outskirts of the city reduce pressure in residential neighborhoods. ONS.Vlaardingen chooses practical solutions instead of parking chaos.

239799.explanation: Vlaardingen should make better use of its location on the water. ONS.Vlaardingen wants to invest in a Waterbus connection (and water taxi) to make the city accessible, attractive, and regionally connected.

239800.explanation: A night metro increases accessibility and safety for workers and nightlife visitors. ONS.Vlaardingen wants the municipality to actively lobby for this within the Metropolitan Region.

239801.explanation: Good bicycle parking prevents theft and makes shopping more accessible. ONS.Vlaardingen sees free, supervised parking as a practical facility that helps residents.

239802.explanation: ONS.Vlaardingen chooses traffic safety. The ring road around the center is not designed for 70 km/h and lies within the built-up area. Serious accidents have occurred on these roads. Driving faster increases risk and yields little benefit. Safety comes first.

239803.explanation: Vlaardingen, as a central municipality, already shelters many homeless people. ONS.Vlaardingen believes the pressure cannot be further increased.

239804.explanation: Neighborhood centers are the heart of the community. ONS.Vlaardingen wants a place for meeting, activities, and support in every neighborhood to strengthen social cohesion and help residents find each other.

239805.explanation: Everyone must be able to receive the care they need. ONS.Vlaardingen chooses accessible and humane care so that residents get help on time and do not fall through the cracks.

239807.explanation: Not more paperwork, but better help. ONS.Vlaardingen wants faster youth care, shorter waiting lists, and fixed caregivers. If extra resources are needed to help children immediately, then that must be done.

239808.explanation: Informal caregivers deserve support. Those who stand ready day and night for others demonstrate humanity and save society a lot of money. ONS.Vlaardingen believes this commitment must be supported with sufficient care and respite.

239747.explanation: Residents must have direct influence on important decisions.

239748.explanation: Good plans from residents always deserve attention and support.

239749.explanation: A good administrator must already pay attention to the elderly in the city.

239750.explanation: Due to temporary housing (labor migrants), societal costs in Vlaardingen have increased.

239751.explanation: Good entrepreneurship must be given space in the city.

239752.explanation: Vlaardingen has a lot to offer as a city. But many people outside the city are not aware of this.

239753.explanation: We do support combating vacancy, but owners must have the chance to find a new tenant. Six months is therefore very short. It makes more sense to do this after 12 months.

239754.explanation: It is important that everyone who needs it receives guidance towards an independent and self-sufficient life.

239755.explanation: The SP opposes age discrimination and prefers to focus on the qualities of individuals.

239757.explanation: The emissions from fireplaces and wood stoves in a densely populated area currently cause many health problems.

239758.explanation: It can also be in the form of materials.

239759.explanation: Sustainability is a broad concept. There is a lot of old industry located in Vlaardingen. Like the residents, it must also become more sustainable.

239761.explanation: It is not a municipal task to co-finance profitable companies. If the municipality had to do this, we would establish a municipal energy company, as is already done abroad.

239762.explanation: Currently, abolishing this scheme is not possible due to national legislation. But everyone should have equal opportunities. If more social rental homes are built, it won’t be necessary to create priority rules.

239763.explanation: Especially concerning the language disadvantage many newcomers face. Integration happens through language.

239764.explanation: This neighborhood was set up to shelter refugees from war. As a society, we must remain open to this. This neighborhood was established by national politics. Vlaardingen cannot simply close it.

239765.explanation: Vlaardingen must comply with the Dispersion Act. Accommodating 100 asylum seekers should therefore not be a problem. The issue is that we are overwhelmed by labor migrants placed in neighborhoods by employment agencies, which strains social costs and capacity.

239767.explanation: Currently, there is already sufficient availability of vocational education (MBO) in the region.

239768.explanation: We must strive to give everyone equal opportunities. Learning disadvantages can thus be eliminated, offering more future opportunities.

239769.explanation: We aim for a healthy lifestyle. Sports clubs have an important role and duty to set a good example.

239771.explanation: The sale of soft drugs is not prohibited and can therefore only be limited to a certain extent. However, coffeeshops near schools should be prevented and people with problems must be helped.

239772.explanation: Important for evidence collection in cases of violence and to support emergency workers on the street.

239773.explanation: It is easy to modify a fatbike for higher speeds. This endangers traffic safety.

239774.explanation: Experience shows that weapons are regularly found. Therefore, it is important to carry out regular checks.

239775.explanation: The municipality should not interfere with pricing in the hospitality sector.

239777.explanation: The demand for social rental housing is currently huge. Therefore, the SP wants more social rental homes.

239778.explanation: There is housing shortage and limited space, so we must build upwards.

239779.explanation: No need to provide subsidies. Developers earn enough.

239780.explanation: There is currently high demand for these apartments but unfortunately little supply.

239781.explanation: There is a housing shortage, and it can help the city center attract better shops and hospitality. Besides more housing, homes rented to labor migrants should become available to regular housing seekers.

239782.explanation: The city's development never stands still. In addition, there is a large housing shortage.

239783.explanation: The Liesveldviaduct is an important traffic artery for visitors, residents, public transport, emergency services, and shop suppliers.

239784.explanation: There is a great housing shortage. This location currently has a lot of old and low-quality industry. Housing is much more desirable here.

239785.explanation: We must fight poverty to create more equal opportunities. Especially children should have the chance to develop, play sports, and participate equally.

239786.explanation: However, full work must be matched with a full wage.

239787.explanation: Due to labor migration, long-term unemployed find it harder to get a job because they must compete with an endless supply of young, strong, highly educated but cheap labor. Regulating labor migration increases the chances for the unemployed to find a job without municipal subsidies.

239788.explanation: Debt forgiveness is an important measure against poverty and for greater livelihood security.

239789.explanation: People aged 55 are by no means written off.

239790.explanation: There are also people who work and provide informal care. So informal caregiving doesn't have to prevent having a job.

239791.explanation: Youth work is important both for the development of young people and for reducing nuisance.

239792.explanation: Free public transport for the elderly reduces loneliness and increases independence.

239793.explanation: Many Vlaardingen residents depend on a car.

239794.explanation: To distribute scarcity fairly and reduce parking space, regulation is a good tool. The first resident parking permit should be free.

239795.explanation: This is an expensive measure that yields little. It is better to await national policy.

239796.explanation: The number of electric cars is increasing, so the number of charging points must also increase.

239797.explanation: Especially on through roads – such as Burgemeester Heusdenslaan and Deltaweg – a speed of 50 km/h must be possible.

239798.explanation: More parking spaces must be created anyway, where there is high parking pressure.

239799.explanation: We support more public transport, including over water.

239800.explanation: Important for outgoing young people and working people.

239801.explanation: At this moment, there is no reason to spend money on free supervised bicycle parking.

239803.explanation: There is always a shortage of shelter places, even emergency beds. Therefore, more places are desperately needed.

239804.explanation: To support the neighborhood and the residents, and the social cohesion.

239805.explanation: Care that falls within municipal tasks — such as youth care and domestic help — requires more quality and attention.

239807.explanation: There are currently enormous waiting lists in youth care. More must also be done on prevention.

239808.explanation: If informal caregivers are (threatened to be) overburdened, this can be a good means to continue guaranteeing care.

239747.explanation: With a real choice including the associated consequences of whether or not to implement the referendum subject.

239748.explanation: The plan must of course comply with certain requirements and national laws and regulations.

239749.explanation: An alderman is responsible for multiple subjects. Just as we have a separate subject for youth care, there must certainly be a subject for elderly policy for which one of the aldermen will be responsible.

239753.explanation: Besides fines, the municipality should steer on shop offerings. Simply imposing fines creates yet another hairdresser or nail studio, and no one wants that.

239757.explanation: We will not prohibit a fire basket/stove or barbecue in your own garden!

239763.explanation: The national government has transferred this task to the municipality. Money received for this must be spent on it. No more.

239764.explanation: We have actually arranged that from March 2027 Vlaardingers can also temporarily live in Mrija.

239765.explanation: Leefbaar was, is, and remains against an asylum seekers’ center in the city.

239769.explanation: Alcohol use under 18 is already legally prohibited.

239777.explanation: In every neighborhood there must be a good distribution of all housing types in all price segments.

239778.explanation: To solve the housing shortage, building upwards will be necessary. But careful consideration must be given to where this is possible.

239779.explanation: No subsidies should be granted to property owners who then sell homes at high prices.

239782.explanation: Stopping building causes an increase in housing shortage. Map out well where building can take place. The Broekpolder remains green!

239783.explanation: The city center must remain easily accessible by car and there must be sufficient parking spaces. Traffic plans and studies must demonstrate that this is guaranteed before any further decisions are made.

239785.explanation: Currently, many arrangements are available both locally and nationally. Map out well where support is needed, so that working pays off but help is offered to those who really need it.

239786.explanation: The primary goal must be that the person starts working. When that fails, that person can still contribute to society.

239788.explanation: This falls under poverty reduction and should therefore be resolved within those budgets.

239789.explanation: Exemption from the job application obligation should not be based on age, but only deployed based on customized solutions.

239790.explanation: Many Vlaardingers provide informal care alongside their work. In some cases, customized solutions could be considered.

239794.explanation: The cause of parking pressure must be investigated. Regulating parking pressure can also be done in other ways, for example with a blue parking disc.

239796.explanation: Make it easy to charge from your own solar panels.

239797.explanation: Do you see everyone driving 30 km/h on Burgemeester Heusdenlaan? 30 where appropriate is fine, but not throughout the entire city.

239803.explanation: More smart solutions that do not necessarily require money.

239747.explanation: We find it important to involve residents better in decisions through participation, where you can provide more input than just “for” or “against.” For complex subjects, the CDA favors establishing a strategic advisory council with residents.

239748.explanation: We are convinced that residents know best what is needed in a neighborhood and can best carry it out. The CDA wants to embrace and give more space to these initiatives in the future.

239749.explanation: Well-being for the elderly is reflected in many subjects and therefore deserves the attention of all aldermen. Only in this way can we guarantee the interests of the elderly on all subjects.

239750.explanation: With the tourist tax, people from outside Vlaardingen contribute to the city, allowing us to keep other taxes, such as property tax, low. The proceeds from the tourist tax pay for the promotion of Vlaardingen.

239751.explanation: The current rules already allow a lot of flexibility in opening hours. To prevent nuisance in the neighborhood, the CDA believes we should not loosen these rules.

239752.explanation: As CDA, we find an exciting and vibrant city important. This is better achieved if we attract more tourists who also spend money in Vlaardingen.

239753.explanation: In the city center of Vlaardingen, too many shops are vacant. The municipality is doing a lot to make the city center attractive again. In return, we expect shop owners to make efforts to rent out the empty shops.

239754.explanation: The CDA believes it is important that everyone who can work does so, but it is currently not necessary to hire more staff for this.

239755.explanation: The CDA believes that the most suitable person should be hired for a job. Age is not a factor, but of course we do consider the experience someone brings.

239757.explanation: The CDA allows residents to choose as much as possible how they want to heat their home. For some people, this is a good alternative for heating their home.

239758.explanation: We are convinced that residents know best what is needed in a neighborhood and can best carry it out. The CDA wants to embrace and give more space to these initiatives in the future.

239759.explanation: It is important for Vlaardingen to attract different types of companies. It is desirable that such companies are as sustainable as possible, but this is not a strict requirement imposed by the municipality.

239761.explanation: The municipality has the task of making a careful consideration together with the residents of a neighborhood for or against a heating network. Money is needed to make this consideration. Ultimately, the heating network must be installed by a company.

239762.explanation: The CDA sees that many people have difficulty finding a rental home. However, we are required to annually house a number of status holders. The CDA is open to doing this differently, but as long as that is not possible, we will adhere to the current system.

239763.explanation: For the CDA, it is important that refugees integrate well into Vlaardingen society. We try to do this as well as possible with the money we currently spend on this annually.

239764.explanation: Mrija is an example of good care for war refugees.

239765.explanation: The CDA understands that the people of Vlaardingen are concerned about the reception of refugees. The municipality must take measures to alleviate these concerns as much as possible, but Vlaardingen must fulfill its legal duty to accommodate refugees.

239767.explanation: There are many different good vocational (MBO) courses. The CDA thinks it is important that Vlaardingen is promoted as an MBO city. We want to attract more MBO courses to Vlaardingen for this purpose.

239768.explanation: This is already happening in the program 'Wij, de Westwijk'. By giving children an extra boost as early as possible, bigger problems in the future are prevented.

239769.explanation: The municipality must encourage that there is no alcohol abuse in sports clubs. We will talk to sports clubs about this, but it is also the responsibility of the athletes themselves.

239771.explanation: The CDA does not want to encourage drug use. The CDA wants to introduce the Local Drug Use Scanner. This scanner maps the drug problems in the city.

239772.explanation: The CDA supports the use of cameras in places where there is a lot of nuisance. This use must match the severity of the nuisance and comply with the privacy protection rules.

239773.explanation: The fatbike causes discomfort and dangerous situations on cycling and walking paths. For the CDA, it is not specifically about banning fatbikes, but mainly about addressing inappropriate behavior.

239774.explanation: Body searches at risk locations are a good approach to preventing crime. The decision must meet legal requirements.

239775.explanation: The CDA believes the municipality should not determine pricing in the hospitality industry. However, the hospitality sector does have a role in the responsible use of alcohol by visitors. The municipality can better guide this.

239776.explanation: The municipality must continue to actively check for placing waste next to containers, but using cameras is too heavy a measure to monitor this.

239777.explanation: Vlaardingen already has many more social rental homes than other cities. There is also a high demand for affordable owner-occupied homes: the CDA thinks that now there can be somewhat more emphasis on that. This way, tenants can buy homes and leave affordable rental housing behind for new tenants.

239778.explanation: Space in Vlaardingen is very limited, so high-rise takes precedence over low-rise. We like to build apartment buildings, interspersed with green areas where residents can recreate and children can play. These green areas ensure a pleasant living and residential environment.

239779.explanation: The CDA would like to stimulate this, but we do not need to pay for it. Property owners profit from converting office buildings.

239780.explanation: The CDA thinks it is important that there is sufficient good housing for the elderly, which can be made suitable for receiving care if necessary. When elderly people move, they often leave behind a nice home for a family.

239781.explanation: To create an exciting and vibrant city center, it is desirable to increase the number of homes. The condition is that these fit well in the city center.

239782.explanation: We cannot build more homes in Vlaardingen than space allows. If there is available space, we can still build homes after 2030.

239783.explanation: The CDA understands the concerns about the accessibility of the city center. Accessibility must be guaranteed, but closing the Liesveld viaduct provides much space to renew the city center. The CDA supports this.

239784.explanation: Groot Vettenoord is located in the middle of the city and it would therefore be suitable to build a beautiful residential area there. But this is a development that cannot be realized in the short term.

239785.explanation: In Vlaardingen, over 2,500 people live below the poverty line. For people dealing with poverty, poverty reduction must be more strongly addressed. The CDA believes that all people – especially children – should be able to participate in society, even if they live in poverty.

239786.explanation: As CDA, we believe it is important that everyone participates. Social activities often provide satisfaction and can be a path to paid employment, but we can never force someone to do this.

239787.explanation: Subsidized jobs help people make the transition back to regular employment: The CDA wants to focus on this.

239788.explanation: The CDA believes that in exceptional cases it is good that taxes can be waived.

239789.explanation: The CDA sees that finding a job at an older age is much harder and that is not fair. At 55, retirement is still far away and we do not want people to stop participating in the labor market then.

239790.explanation: The CDA highly appreciates informal caregivers and they should be able to count on municipal support. As an informal caregiver, you must be able to continue participating in society and having a job is important in this.

239791.explanation: The CDA believes that we must invest in youth work, because this can prevent bigger problems in the future.

239792.explanation: In Vlaardingen, there is already free public transport for elderly people with a low income. Elderly people with a good pension are well able to buy a ticket for public transport themselves.

239793.explanation: We want to encourage residents to come to the city by bike or public transport. However, it is important that the city center remains easily accessible by car. We have several parking garages for this.

239794.explanation: In some neighborhoods, parking pressure is very high. This can only be partially solved with paid parking. The CDA supports adding parking spaces on the edge of the neighborhood to reduce parking pressure in this way.

239795.explanation: The CDA believes that air pollution must be tackled. Introducing an environmental zone is expensive and has little effect. More and more cars are becoming electric, which naturally improves air quality. Therefore, the CDA does not find an environmental zone in the center of Vlaardingen necessary.

239796.explanation: The number of electric vehicles and the number of charging points must remain balanced so that parking spaces are optimally used.

239797.explanation: To limit nuisance and increase the safety of cyclists and pedestrians, the CDA supports lowering the speed limit to 30 km/h on more roads.

239798.explanation: The CDA believes that parking hubs are necessary to solve the parking problems in Vlaardingen. Fewer cars in the neighborhood make living there more attractive.

239799.explanation: With the construction of many new homes in the river zone, the connection to the Waterbus becomes more attractive.

239800.explanation: Many people in our region work outside regular office hours: in the port or in hospitality. It is important that they can get to work on time or get home in the evening. Therefore, the CDA believes the metro should run more frequently at night.

239801.explanation: The CDA wants to encourage residents to cycle to shopping centers. It is nice to be able to park your bike in a guarded bike shed.

239802.explanation: Increasing the maximum speed limit can only be done on a very limited number of roads. The CDA is not in favor of this because the chance of accidents increases and more nuisance arises.

239803.explanation: The CDA believes that the municipality must invest in sufficient shelters for homeless and roofless people. Everyone deserves a safe place to sleep. If the current shelter capacity is insufficient, the municipality should allocate extra funds to create more space. This way, we ensure that no one falls between the cracks.

239804.explanation: The CDA finds it important that every neighborhood has a place where people can meet each other. This combats loneliness and strengthens social networks.

239805.explanation: Healthcare is funded by the national government. Unfortunately, the municipality cannot allocate more money for this. The CDA does believe that more national funding is needed for sufficient care.

239807.explanation: Unfortunately, we see that more and more children and young people need youth care. The CDA believes that youth care should act more preventively to control costs.

239808.explanation: Providing long-term informal care can be very demanding. To sustain this, it is sometimes necessary to be able to recover. We must arrange this to ensure the loving help of caregivers remains sustainable.

239747.explanation: D66 wants citizen forums and neighborhood panels so residents can think along early about policies that affect their living environment.

239748.explanation: Initiatives from residents increase involvement and lead to better plans.

239749.explanation: Policy for the elderly touches on housing, education, care, and work. This requires shared responsibility, not just one separate portfolio.

239750.explanation: This is negotiable if it demonstrably contributes to facilities and does not make the city less attractive.

239751.explanation: Freedom where possible, with clear agreements to prevent nuisance for local residents.

239752.explanation: We must primarily invest now in our own residents. Once that is successful, we can attract tourists.

239753.explanation: A vacancy regulation works well because owners are encouraged to use or rent out properties faster. This prevents decay, attracts new residents and entrepreneurs, and keeps city or village centers lively and attractive.

239754.explanation: More staff provide more personal and faster guidance, helping welfare recipients be directed to work more effectively. This increases income opportunities, reduces long-term dependency, and strengthens residents’ self-reliance.

239755.explanation: D66 sees the major shortages in the labor market. The municipality of Vlaardingen also has many unfilled vacancies. The municipality must do its best to fill these. People over 50 can play an important role in this.

239757.explanation: Allowing open fireplaces and wood stoves under all circumstances increases air pollution and health risks, contributes to CO₂ emissions and fine dust, and undermines the city's livability, especially for vulnerable groups such as children and the elderly.

239758.explanation: By making funds available, residents can maintain greenery themselves, making the neighborhood more beautiful and livable. This stimulates involvement, social contacts, and ownership, and results in more greenery, biodiversity, and a pleasant living environment for everyone.

239759.explanation: Allowing only sustainable companies promotes clean production, limits environmental damage, and encourages innovation. At the same time, we cannot yet require all companies to operate this way.

239761.explanation: Municipal contributions to district heating accelerate the transition to sustainable heat, reduce CO₂ emissions, and make sustainable energy more affordable for residents. It stimulates innovation, reduces dependence on fossil fuels, and strengthens the local energy supply.

239762.explanation: Maintaining priority for refugees helps them quickly find safe housing, prevents homelessness, and supports integration. With a stable home, they can work, learn, and participate. Vlaardingen must take responsibility for this, as other municipalities do.

239763.explanation: More funding for integration helps newcomers learn the language and culture faster, find work, and build social contacts. This increases their independence, strengthens society, and reduces social problems in the long term.

239764.explanation: Mrija is an important shelter for Ukrainians. We are proud to provide shelter to this group.

239765.explanation: D66 adheres to the Spread Law. We must shelter 100 people. This has to happen somewhere and no place is perfect. The Zuidbuurtseweg is an acceptable location.

239767.explanation: We already have a good offer of vocational education (MBO). We would like to add more programs.

239768.explanation: Guidance helps to give children with a disadvantage equal opportunities.

239769.explanation: The municipality can make agreements with sports clubs to limit alcohol use. This contributes to a healthy sports environment, sets a good example for young people, and prevents alcohol use from being seen as a normal part of sports and leisure.

239771.explanation: D66 supports the legalization of soft drugs. Coffeeshops are not the ideal way of soft drug use, but we are not against new coffeeshops.

239772.explanation: More camera surveillance limits residents' privacy, can lead to a feeling of constant control, and does not always effectively solve crimes. It can undermine trust between residents and government and replaces preventive measures such as neighborhood safety and social involvement.

239773.explanation: The nuisance caused by fatbikes is significant. Enforcement is difficult but very important.

239774.explanation: At this moment, all of Vlaardingen is designated as a safety risk area. This means preventive searches are allowed throughout Vlaardingen. This is unnecessary, is too intrusive on privacy, and makes too many Vlaardingers suspects by default.

239775.explanation: Alcohol is unhealthy. Nonetheless, it is a free choice to drink it and to market it in this way. From a health perspective, we are against it, but we support giving entrepreneurs the freedom to run their businesses in their own way.

239776.explanation: Cameras at waste containers constitute too great an intrusion on the privacy of Vlaardingen residents and are legally not permitted. To prevent waste next to containers, the municipality should focus on better information, sufficient capacity, frequent emptying, and clear enforcement without compromising privacy.

239777.explanation: D66 finds it important that there are sufficient social rental homes. Currently, the number of social rental homes in the region is unevenly distributed. Vlaardingen has many, while other municipalities in the region have very few. We will correct this by primarily building affordable homes in Vlaardingen.

239778.explanation: To solve the housing shortage, we must build more. The places where this can happen in Vlaardingen are limited, but for example in the city center and around public transport hubs, we can compact well and build more apartments.

239779.explanation: This way, we kill two birds with one stone: more homes and less deteriorating empty offices.

239780.explanation: To promote flow in the housing market, we must also build homes for the elderly, such as cohousing communities.

239781.explanation: D66 wants to build more homes, including in the city center.

239782.explanation: D66 wants to build more homes to address the housing shortage.

239783.explanation: We support the ambitious plans for the city center. This also includes a beautiful green city park. With these plans, we make the city center attractive again and build additional homes.

239784.explanation: D66 wants to build more homes. However, the province requires us to maintain the same amount of square meters of industrial land. We can build here, but then we must develop another industrial area elsewhere in the city. We are willing to do this.

239785.explanation: Spending less money on people with low incomes increases poverty, widens social inequality, and can lead to more societal problems. It reduces opportunities for education, work, and health, causing vulnerable residents to fall even further behind.

239786.explanation: Encouraging people on social assistance benefits to participate in community work promotes commitment, structure, and experience. It increases their chances of employment, strengthens the community, and prevents long-term dependency on benefits.

239787.explanation: Providing financial support to companies that hire long-term unemployed people increases the chance of employment, shortens the duration of benefit dependency, and encourages employers to be more inclusive, which strengthens the local economy and society.

239788.explanation: This increases financial pressure, can lead to (more) debt and poverty, and makes it harder for vulnerable groups to make ends meet and live independently.

239789.explanation: This can reduce their chances of work, limit their independence, and increase the risk of long-term benefit dependency. Actively continuing the search keeps them engaged and increases job opportunities.

239790.explanation: This can discourage them from actively looking for work, limit their independence, and reduce the chance that they eventually leave social assistance. Active participation remains important.

239791.explanation: Cutting youth work reduces support and guidance for young people, increases the risk of school dropout, social problems, and nuisance. Good youth work prevents problems, stimulates development, and strengthens involvement in society.

239792.explanation: Currently, public transport is free for elderly people up to 120% of the social minimum. This helps elderly people with limited income. Free public transport for all elderly would also help those who do not need it. D66 prefers to invest that money elsewhere.

239793.explanation: D66 wants to build extra homes, also in the center. This means there must also be additional parking spaces.

239794.explanation: D66 believes that paid parking is not an end in itself. Based on residents' wishes, parking pressure, and the design of the neighborhood, it can be decided whether paid parking is an appropriate measure. Tailored solutions are necessary.

239795.explanation: By banning the most polluting trucks and vans, we keep the air in the city center cleaner, which improves the health of our residents. Cars can still drive in the city center anyway.

239796.explanation: Stopping the designation of charging points limits opportunities for electric cars, hinders sustainable mobility, and may discourage residents from switching to clean transportation, which would increase CO₂ emissions and air pollution.

239797.explanation: A maximum speed of 30 km/h within built-up areas increases traffic safety, reduces noise and emissions, and makes streets safer for cyclists and pedestrians. On some busy or wide main roads, 50 km/h may remain allowed to maintain traffic flow.

239798.explanation: We want to build more homes. That means there must also be parking spaces. However, parking on the outskirts of the city is not attractive. Parking can be located on the edge of the neighborhood, making the neighborhood low-traffic.

239799.explanation: Investing in a connection to the Waterbus improves Vlaardingen's accessibility, stimulates sustainable transport, and attracts visitors and businesses. It offers residents more mobility and increases the city's economic and tourism opportunities.

239800.explanation: Night metro rides between Vlaardingen and Rotterdam increase mobility for work, study, and leisure, reduce car use and traffic pressure, and make public transport more attractive, safer, and sustainable for residents and visitors.

239801.explanation: Free bicycle parking ensures that people can more easily do their shopping by bike.

239802.explanation: This causes more noise nuisance, air pollution, and is less safe.

239803.explanation: With the current winter shelter, there seems to be enough space available and even for more months of the year. We first want to wait and see if this proves sufficient in the future before creating more space.

239804.explanation: D66 considers neighborhood centers important as meeting places in the community. Every neighborhood has one, but they are not always well known. By making them more visible and, where possible, combining them with functions such as education, space is used more efficiently and more residents benefit from these centers.

239805.explanation: The municipality should make more funds available for people who need care.

239807.explanation: More funding for youth care helps children timely with problems, increases their development chances, and prevents escalation. It strengthens families and contributes to a safe society. This is only possible if the national government provides sufficient funds, which so far has been insufficient.

239808.explanation: How this can be made possible is if more national funding is made available from the government. Then it is a welcome moment of rest for caregivers, who are being asked for so much.

239747.explanation: The municipal council has the task, as representatives of the people, to make decisions for the entire city. In case of changes in a neighborhood, communication, participation, and consultation with residents are important.

239748.explanation: Residents must have the opportunity to shape their own environment. ChristenUnie-SGP Vlaardingen wants to make funds available to support residents’ initiatives.

239749.explanation: Every alderman must be able to serve every resident of Vlaardingen. All age groups are affected by all policy areas. It is not desirable to shift this responsibility to only one alderman.

239750.explanation: Vlaardingen must be an attractive city for tourists to visit. This means that the tax must be balanced with spending on tourism. Increasing it is possible, but it must be clear what the money will be spent on.

239751.explanation: Hospitality venues may determine their opening hours within set frameworks. This prevents nuisance in residential neighborhoods and keeps Vlaardingen livable.

239752.explanation: ChristenUnie-SGP believes that enough money is invested in promoting Vlaardingen. Every euro can only be spent once.

239753.explanation: Investing in shopping centers requires cooperation between the municipality, shopkeepers, and property owners. Imposing vacancy fines leads to meaningless use of retail spaces and does not contribute to a solution.

239754.explanation: The municipality has a staff shortage. Many people are also needed in other sectors. Therefore, this is not a priority at this time.

239755.explanation: Every candidate should be assessed based on their abilities, not who they are. Civil service organizations already employ a relatively large number of people over 50, which will lead to a wave of retirements in the coming years.

239757.explanation: The quality of the air we breathe is important and deserves attention. That is why subsidies exist to remove chimneys. However, a burning ban is difficult to enforce. We are not in favor of a ban, but the municipality must inform residents about the Burning Guide.

239758.explanation: Residents must have the opportunity to shape their own environment. ChristenUnie-SGP Vlaardingen wants to make funds available to support residents’ initiatives.

239759.explanation: Sustainability is important, also concerning businesses. However, this especially affects small Vlaardingen entrepreneurs. Large companies can invest to meet possible requirements, which small Vlaardingen businesses find more difficult. Also, this is not enforceable after establishment.

239761.explanation: In sustainability efforts, a locally appropriate and affordable solution must be found for every part of Vlaardingen. A heat network can play a role here. The municipality has a pioneering role in this, but it is not a given that municipal subsidies should always be spent on this.

239762.explanation: Status holders make up a small part of social housing applications. Granting them priority therefore costs the municipality little. At the same time, it is undesirable for people to stay unnecessarily in refugee shelters. Therefore, these people must quickly receive housing.

239763.explanation: ChristenUnie-SGP wants to actively focus on integration through meetings and activities.

239764.explanation: Mrija shelters people from war zones. Until the new water supply is constructed, this piece of land is not needed and can therefore be very well used for the accommodation of these people.

239765.explanation: While different opinions exist regarding national policy on asylum and migration, the local task remains to accommodate people who are legally here; also for Vlaardingen. The Zuidbuurtseweg location has been deemed suitable for this.

239767.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP has actively worked on this theme in the past period and will continue to do so. Training the next generation of skilled workers is important, especially because fewer young people are opting for practical education.

239768.explanation: The responsibility for raising children lies primarily with the parents and then with the school. Sometimes extra support can help. The home situation should not hinder children's development. The municipality should not organize this but facilitate it.

239769.explanation: The municipality imposes no obligations on sports clubs but encourages a healthy lifestyle.

239771.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP is in favor of closing the existing coffeeshops.

statements.title: A reception center for asylum seekers may be located on Zuidbuurtseweg.
statements.moreInfo: Under the Distribution Act Vlaardingen must provide 100 reception places for asylum seekers. Initially that was 351, but because the municipality also accommodates many Ukrainians the number was set at 100. The college of mayor and aldermen has designated Zuidbuurtseweg 1 as the reception location. The municipal council has approved this. Research shows that there are probably protected animals (bats) at the location. The preparations have therefore been delayed.

statements.title: Vlaardingen must appoint an alderman who focuses solely on policies for the elderly.
statements.moreInfo: Based on its population Vlaardingen may have 7 aldermen. In the past Vlaardingen usually had 5 aldermen. At the moment Vlaardingen has 6 aldermen. The aldermen and the mayor divide the municipality’s tasks among themselves. If there remain 6 aldermen and one of them focuses only on elderly affairs, the other aldermen and the mayor will take on more other subjects. A 7th full-time alderman costs about €120,000 extra per year.

statements.title: Owners of shops that have been empty for more than six months must receive a fine from the municipality.
The Ukrainian neighborhood Mrija must be closed as soon as possible.
statements.moreInfo: In Vlaardingen there is a temporary neighborhood with almost 400 flexible homes. More than 1,000 Ukrainian refugees live there. The neighborhood was built on land intended for businesses and industry. The municipality receives funds from the national government for this temporary reception. The reception was originally intended to remain until 2026. In July 2025 the municipal council decided to extend the reception until 2030 for about 750–800 Ukrainian refugees. It was also decided to rent the remaining homes until 2030 to other housing seekers.

statements.moreInfo: Municipalities are actually only responsible for school buildings. Educational institutions decide themselves where they offer which programs. A municipality can influence which programs are offered locally, for example by lobbying school boards. Since 2022 Vlaardingen has been promoting the "Vlaardingen mbo-stad" program. The program currently focuses on the sectors food, care and technology.
