Vlaardingen must allocate money to obtain a connection to the Waterbus.
The municipality must place cameras at waste containers.
The municipality must introduce paid parking in more neighborhoods.
A new residential neighborhood must be built on the Groot Vettenoord business park.
statements.moreInfo: Groot Vettenoord is an outdated business park on the east side of the city. The area can be redeveloped into a more attractive business park. Housing can also be built there.

statements.title: The municipality must co-fund the construction of district heating networks.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meebetalen aan de aanleg van

titleParts.glossary: Network of underground pipes that brings hot water from a central source to homes and buildings, for both heating and tap water.

statements.title: People receiving welfare benefits who provide informal care to a family member or acquaintance do not have to apply for jobs.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

titleParts.text: die een familielid of bekende verzorgen als

titleParts.glossary: Someone who provides unpaid and often long-term care for sick family members or friends.

statements.title: The municipality must not designate any more parking spaces as charging spots for electric cars.
Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
Every neighborhood in the city must have a community center.

shootoutStatements.theme: Exemption from the job application obligation
shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should exempt people over 55 on welfare from the job-application requirement.
The municipality should make funds available to citizens who want to maintain the greenery in their neighborhood themselves.
The municipality should not continue to give refugees with residence permits priority for social rental housing.
The municipality should make more funds available for people who need care.
Residents who have a plan to improve their neighborhood should be able to receive funding from the municipality.
The Liesveld viaduct should remain a through road for cars.
shootoutStatements.moreInfo: Het Liesveldviaduct loopt dwars door de stad. De gemeente wil de binnenstad levendig en aantrekkelijk maken voor inwoners en bezoekers. Er is een plan om het Liesveldviaduct af te sluiten voor doorgaand verkeer en hier een stadspark te maken. Ook moeten er nieuwe woningen komen. Tegenstanders denken dat de binnenstad dan niet meer goed te bereiken is met de auto.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should give companies money to hire long-term unemployed people.
More high residential towers should be built in Vlaardingen.
Vlaardingen should increase surveillance by cameras.
Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.
Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) should check fatbike riders more often.
The municipality should spend less money supporting low-income people.
The tourist tax should be increased.
More apartments for elderly people should be built in Vlaardingen.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.
The municipality should allocate more money to youth care.
No new homes may be built in Vlaardingen after 2030.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.
The municipality should hire more staff to guide welfare recipients into work.
The municipality should lobby the Rotterdam-The Hague Metropolitan Region to have the metro run to and from Rotterdam at night.
The municipality should cut spending on youth work.
The municipality should give money to Vlaardingen schools so they can help children after school with extra lessons and homework support.
The municipality should make more funds available for temporary replacement care so informal caregivers can recuperate.
The speed limit on the ring road around the center, such as Burgemeester Heusdenslaan, may be raised to 70 km/h.
Vlaardingen should introduce a low-emission (environmental) zone in the city center.
There should be fewer parking spaces in the city centre.
Free supervised bicycle parking should be provided at the shopping centres in Westwijk and Holy.
